system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.

<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [momentumTransport, physicalProperties]</dir>
</directory_structure></similar_case_reference>
<similar_case_advice>match_level='high' use_scope="Use the matched pitzDaily/incompressible/RAS/pimpleFoam case as the primary template for all files including 0/ (boundary conditions), constant/ (transportProperties, turbulenceProperties, polyMesh), and system/ (controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution); adjust transportProperties (nu 1e-05), controlDict (deltaT 0.0001, writeInterval 0.01, endTime 0.3), and boundary condition values (inlet U 15 m/s, outlet pressure zeroGradient, wall noSlip) to match the user's specifications." advice='Directly adopt the matched pitzDaily RAS/pimpleFoam tutorial structure and modify only the transportProperties nu value to 1e-05, update controlDict timing parameters (deltaT=0.0001, writeInterval=0.01, endTime=0.3), and set the inlet velocity magnitude to 15 m/s while keeping the existing mesh, turbulence model setup, schemes, and PIMPLE solver settings intact.'</similar_case_advice>
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
keyword div(phi,epsilon) is undefined in dictionary ".../system/fvSchemes/divSchemes"
```

The `pimpleFoam` solver requires a divergence scheme for `div(phi,epsilon)`, but the current `fvSchemes` file does not define it. Looking at the current `divSchemes`:

```
default         none;
div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
```

There is no entry for `div(phi,epsilon)`. Since `default` is set to `none`, any undefined divergence scheme will cause a fatal error.

## Solution

Add the missing `div(phi,epsilon)` entry to the `divSchemes` sub-dictionary in `system/fvSchemes`. Based on the reference pitzDaily tutorial case, a reasonable approach would be to add:

```
div(phi,epsilon)  Gauss linear;
```

This follows the typical pattern used in the pitzDaily tutorial where the diffusion of turbulent kinetic energy (`epsilon`) is handled with a `Gauss linear` scheme. Alternatively, since `default` is already set to `Gauss linear` in the `laplacianSchemes` section, another valid option would be to either:

1. Set `default` in `divSchemes` to `Gauss linear;` (which would catch any unspecified divergence terms), **or**
2. Add the specific `div(phi,epsilon)` entry as shown above.

Either approach will resolve the immediate error. Option 1 is simpler and more consistent with how the pitzDaily tutorial handles it.
</Review_Analysis>
</Attempt>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-05.</user_requirement>

I have modified the files according to your previous suggestions. If the error persists, please provide further guidance. Make sure your suggestions adhere to user requirements and do not contradict it. Also, please consider the previous attempts and try a different approach.
